Off Calendar May-30-2014 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summ Adjudication
Off Calendar May-22-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James Napoli, Esq., 415.995.5060, and Jillian Somers, Esq., 415.995.5101, for PLAINTIFF CONVALESCENT CENTER, MISSION ST., INC. DBA SAN FRANCISCO NURSING CENTER.
Jennier Addams, Deputy Attorney General, 415.703.5382 and Julia Clayton, Deputy Attorney General, 415.703.5619, for DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA DEPARTMETN OF PUBLIC HEALTH.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling re: PLAINTIFF CONVALESCENT CENTER, MISSION ST., INC. DBA SAN FRANCISCO NURSING CENTER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication. Plaintiff's motion for summary judgment is granted on the grounds that the Defendant cannot establish that the violations alleged in Citation No. 22-1163-0007568-F (the "Citation") were a direct, proximate cause of Barbara M.'s death. The Citation lists five grounds for the violation of 42 C.F.R 483.13(b): (1) negligent monitoring, supervision, and evaluation of Maximo Fajardo's hiring, training, and orientation process; (2) failure to complete/document a Post Training Performance Evaluation; (3) failure to develop a policy and procedure on Post Training Performance Evaluation; (4) failure to conduct an orientation and direction to make sure Fajardo understood the needs of his residents; and (5) failure to check on Fajardo because it was bathing day for the residents and another Certified Nursing Assistant was unsure if Fajardo was capable of bathing the patients residents to his care. (Declaration of Jillian M. Somers, Ex. C.) Plaintiff met its initial burden of making a prima facie showing that Defendant cannot provide evidence that the above violations were "a direct proximate cause" of Barbara M.'s death, as required by Cal. Health & Safety Code sec. 1424(c) and 1428(e). (UMF 1-19.) Defendant did not meet its burden of providing evidence establishing that the above violations were a direct proximate cause of Barbara M.'s death. Rather, Defendant argues that there is a triable issue of material fact as to whether additional violations, ie: violations not included in the citation, were a proximate cause of Barbara M.'s death. Defendant cites no authority that permits additional violations to be raised for the first time on appeal of the citation to the Superior Court. Health & Safety Code sec. 1424(b) provides that the Department can raise new facts in support of the amount of the penalty assessed, but it does not provide that new violations may be raised. Additionally, the Court finds that collateral estoppel does not apply because the Statement of Deficiencies was not a final administrative decision. The facts in Murray v. Alaska Airlines, Inc. (2010) 50 Cal.4th 860, relied on by Defendant in support of its collateral estoppel argument, are distinguishable from the facts present here. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Joyce Finley #8620.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-01-2013 Set For Trial On Jun-23-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
